properties.title: Old Stone Church (Congregational Society), from the northwest
properties.desc: Church building at corner of Central & Worcester Sts, West Boylston; documented by Water Board photographer Charles W. Tarr before demolition. Now submerged under the reservoir.
properties.coord_note: Approx. corner of Central & Worcester Sts per photo title; area now beneath reservoir surface

properties.title: Old Stone Church (Congregational Society), from the southeast
properties.desc: Opposite view of the same stone church building; second of two angles photographed on the same day for the Metropolitan Water Board real-estate survey.
properties.coord_note: Approx. corner of Central & Worcester Sts per photo title

properties.title: First Baptist Church, West Boylston
properties.desc: Baptist church in old West Boylston town center, photographed as part of the Metropolitan Water Board property survey. Now submerged.
properties.coord_note: Old West Boylston town area; DC gives town-level centroid only

properties.title: Catholic Church, West Boylston
properties.desc: Catholic church building in old West Boylston, part of the reservoir real-estate acquisition survey.
properties.coord_note: Old West Boylston town area; DC gives town-level centroid only

properties.title: Wachusett Dam — first stone laid
properties.desc: Construction milestone: first masonry stone placed at the dam site. Photographed by George P. Goodman for the Metropolitan Water and Sewerage Board.
properties.coord_note: Wachusett Dam; coordinates from USGS NHD dam feature

properties.title: Construction railroad cut near Sandy Pond, from the north
properties.desc: Railroad grading cut used to haul dam construction materials; photographed near Sandy Pond in Clinton, east of the reservoir basin.
properties.coord_note: Approx. Sandy Pond area per photo title; Clinton MA

properties.title: North Dike construction
properties.desc: Earthwork construction of the North Dike, which closes the northern gap in the reservoir rim between Clinton and Sterling.
properties.coord_note: North Dike crest; approximate from reservoir geometry

properties.title: Building the West Boylston Arch
properties.desc: Stone arch under construction over the Quinapoxet River where it enters the reservoir; photographed by Charles W. Tarr.
properties.coord_note: Quinapoxet River arch, West Boylston; approx. from reservoir shoreline

properties.title: West Boylston Arch, completed
properties.desc: Completed stone arch carrying the former road over the Quinapoxet River at the reservoir inlet — still partially visible above waterline today.
